3: use crate::error::EzError;
4: use crate::git;

6: use crate::stack::StackState;

9: /// Known global config keys and their descriptions.
10: const KNOWN_KEYS: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
11:     ("trunk", "Trunk branch name (e.g. main, master, develop)"),
12:     ("remote", "Default git remote (e.g. origin, fork, upstream)"),
13:     (
14:         "default_from",
15:         "Default parent for `ez create` when on trunk",
16:     ),
17:     ("repo", "GitHub repo for PR operations (owner/name)"),
18:     ("draft", "Default new PRs to draft (true/false)"),
19:     ("no_pr", "Default push to skip PR creation (true/false)"),
20:     ("rerere", "Enable git rerere for conflict recording (true/false)"),
21: ];
22: 
23: /// Known branch attribute keys.
24: const BRANCH_KEYS: &[(&str, &str)] = &[
25:     ("pr", "Set PR (number, owner/repo#N, or URL) — smart parser"),
26:     ("pr_repo", "PR target repository (owner/name)"),
27:     ("pr_number", "PR number"),
28:     ("push_remote", "Git remote to push this branch to"),
29:     ("parent", "Parent branch in the stack"),
30:     ("scope", "File scope patterns (comma-separated)"),
31:     ("scope_mode", "Scope enforcement mode (warn/strict)"),
32: ];
33: 
34: /// Global keys that accept only boolean values.
35: const BOOL_KEYS: &[&str] = &["draft", "no_pr", "rerere"];
36: 
37: fn is_known_global_key(key: &str) -> bool {
38:     KNOWN_KEYS.iter().any(|(k, _)| *k == key)
39: }
40: 
41: fn is_known_branch_key(key: &str) -> bool {
42:     BRANCH_KEYS.iter().any(|(k, _)| *k == key)
43: }
44: 
45: fn is_bool_key(key: &str) -> bool {
46:     BOOL_KEYS.contains(&key)
47: }
48: 
49: /// Parse a user-supplied string as a boolean.
50: /// Accepts: true, false, 1, 0, yes, no (case-insensitive).
51: fn parse_bool(value: &str) -> Result<bool> {
52:     match value.to_lowercase().as_str() {
53:         "true" | "1" | "yes" => Ok(true),
54:         "false" | "0" | "no" => Ok(false),
55:         _ => bail!(EzError::UserMessage(format!(
56:             "invalid boolean value `{value}`\n  → Accepted values: true, false, 1, 0, yes, no"
57:         ))),
58:     }
59: }
60: 
61: /// Resolve which branch name to target.
62: /// `branch_flag` is the raw `--branch` value:
63: ///   - `None` → no --branch flag → use current branch
64: ///   - `Some("")` → bare --branch → use current branch
65: ///   - `Some("feat/x")` → explicit branch name
66: fn resolve_branch(branch_flag: Option<&str>) -> Result<String> {
67:     match branch_flag {
68:         None | Some("") => git::current_branch(),
69:         Some(name) => Ok(name.to_string()),
70:     }
71: }
72: 
73: /// Determine if a key should be treated as per-branch.
74: /// Returns `(effective_key, is_branch)`:
75: ///   - `branch.pr_repo` → `("pr_repo", true)`
76: ///   - `remote --branch` → `("push_remote", true)` (special: global `remote` becomes per-branch `push_remote`)
77: ///   - `pr_repo` → `("pr_repo", true)` (known branch key, no prefix needed)
78: ///   - `trunk` → `("trunk", false)` (global key)
79: fn classify_key(raw_key: &str, branch_flag: Option<&str>) -> (String, bool) {
80:     // 1. Explicit `branch.` prefix
81:     if let Some(stripped) = raw_key.strip_prefix("branch.") {
82:         return (stripped.to_string(), true);
83:     }
84: 
85:     // 2. --branch flag forces branch scope
86:     if branch_flag.is_some() {
87:         // Special case: global `remote` + --branch → per-branch `push_remote`
88:         if raw_key == "remote" {
89:             return ("push_remote".to_string(), true);
90:         }
91:         // If it's a known global key that also makes sense per-branch, still use it
92:         if is_known_branch_key(raw_key) || is_known_global_key(raw_key) {
93:             return (raw_key.to_string(), true);
94:         }
95:         return (raw_key.to_string(), true);
96:     }
97: 
98:     // 3. Known branch attribute → auto-detect as branch-scoped
99:     if is_known_branch_key(raw_key) {
100:         return (raw_key.to_string(), true);
101:     }
102: 
103:     // 4. Global key
104:     (raw_key.to_string(), false)
105: }
106: 
107: // ── PR value parsing ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
108: 
109: /// Parsed PR reference from user input.
110: #[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
111: struct ParsedPr {
112:     repo: Option<String>,
113:     number: Option<u64>,
114: }
115: 
116: /// Smart-parse a PR value. Accepts:
117: ///   - `123` or `#123` → number only
118: ///   - `owner/repo#123` → repo + number
119: ///   - `owner/repo` → repo only
120: ///   - `https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123` → repo + number
121: fn parse_pr_value(value: &str) -> ParsedPr {
122:     let value = value.trim();
123: 
124:     // URL: https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123
125:     if value.starts_with("https://github.com/") || value.starts_with("http://github.com/") {
126:         let cleaned = value
127:             .trim_start_matches("https://github.com/")
128:             .trim_start_matches("http://github.com/");
129:         // expect: owner/repo/pull/123
130:         let parts: Vec<&str> = cleaned.splitn(4, '/').collect();
131:         if parts.len() >= 4 && parts[2] == "pull" {
132:             let repo = format!("{}/{}", parts[0], parts[1]);
133:             let number = parts[3].parse::<u64>().ok();
134:             return ParsedPr {
135:                 repo: Some(repo),
136:                 number,
137:             };
138:         }
139:         // Partial URL — try repo only
140:         if parts.len() >= 2 {
141:             let repo = format!("{}/{}", parts[0], parts[1]);
142:             return ParsedPr {
143:                 repo: Some(repo),
144:                 number: None,
145:             };
146:         }
147:         return ParsedPr {
148:             repo: None,
149:             number: None,
150:         };
151:     }
152: 
153:     // #123
154:     if let Some(stripped) = value.strip_prefix('#') {
155:         return ParsedPr {
156:             repo: None,
157:             number: stripped.parse::<u64>().ok(),
158:         };
159:     }
160: 
161:     // Bare number → pr_number
162:     if let Ok(n) = value.parse::<u64>() {
163:         return ParsedPr {
164:             repo: None,
165:             number: Some(n),
166:         };
167:     }
168: 
169:     // owner/repo#123
170:     if let Some((repo_part, num_part)) = value.split_once('#') {
171:         return ParsedPr {
172:             repo: Some(github::resolve_repo_shorthand(repo_part)),
173:             number: num_part.parse::<u64>().ok(),
174:         };
175:     }
176: 
177:     // owner/repo (contains /)
178:     if value.contains('/') {
179:         return ParsedPr {
180:             repo: Some(value.to_string()),
181:             number: None,
182:         };
183:     }
184: 
185:     // Bare string with no / — can't determine, treat as error
186:     ParsedPr {
187:         repo: None,
188:         number: None,
189:     }
190: }
191: 
192: /// Parse `pr_repo` value. Unlike the smart `pr` parser, this always sets repo.
193: ///   - `123` → literal "123" (repo name is actually a number)
194: ///   - `myrepo` → resolve via shorthand (check remotes, then prepend owner)
195: ///   - `owner/repo` → as-is
196: fn parse_pr_repo_value(value: &str) -> String {
197:     let value = value.trim();
198:     if value.contains('/') {
199:         return value.to_string();
200:     }
201:     // Pure number → literal repo name (don't resolve)
202:     if value.parse::<u64>().is_ok() {
203:         return value.to_string();
204:     }
205:     // Resolve via shorthand chain
206:     github::resolve_repo_shorthand(value)
207: }
208: 
209: // ── Public API ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
210: 
211: pub fn list() -> Result<()> {
212:     let state = StackState::load()?;
213: 
214:     ui::header("ez config");
215:     for (key, description) in KNOWN_KEYS {
216:         let value = get_global_value(&state, key);
217:         let display = match &value {
218:             Some(v) => v.clone(),
219:             None => "(not set)".to_string(),
220:         };
221:         eprintln!("  {key:15} = {display}");
222:         eprintln!("  {}", ui::dim(&format!("  {description}")));
223:     }
224:     Ok(())
225: }
226: 
227: pub fn get(key: &str, branch_flag: Option<&str>) -> Result<()> {
228:     let state = StackState::load()?;
229:     let (effective_key, is_branch) = classify_key(key, branch_flag);
230: 
231:     if is_branch {
232:         let branch_name = resolve_branch(branch_flag)?;
233:         let meta = state.get_branch(&branch_name)?;
234:         match get_branch_value(meta, &effective_key) {
235:             Some(v) => {
236:                 println!("{v}");
237:                 Ok(())
238:             }
239:             None => bail!(EzError::UserMessage(format!(
240:                 "branch `{branch_name}` has no `{effective_key}` set"
241:             ))),
242:         }
243:     } else {
244:         if !is_known_global_key(&effective_key) {
245:             bail!(EzError::UserMessage(format!(
246:                 "unknown config key `{effective_key}`\n  → Known keys: {}\n  → Branch attrs: {}",
247:                 KNOWN_KEYS
248:                     .iter()
249:                     .map(|(k, _)| *k)
250:                     .collect::<Vec<_>>()
251:                     .join(", "),
252:                 BRANCH_KEYS
253:                     .iter()
254:                     .map(|(k, _)| *k)
255:                     .collect::<Vec<_>>()
256:                     .join(", "),
257:             )));
258:         }
259:         match get_global_value(&state, &effective_key) {
260:             Some(v) => {
261:                 println!("{v}");
262:                 Ok(())
263:             }
264:             None => bail!(EzError::UserMessage(format!(
265:                 "config key `{effective_key}` is not set\n  → Set it with: ez config set {effective_key} <value>"
266:             ))),
267:         }
268:     }
269: }
270: 
271: pub fn set(key: &str, value: &str, branch_flag: Option<&str>) -> Result<()> {
272:     let (effective_key, is_branch) = classify_key(key, branch_flag);
273: 
274:     let mut state = StackState::load()?;
275: 
276:     if is_branch {
277:         let branch_name = resolve_branch(branch_flag)?;
278: 
279:         if !state.is_managed(&branch_name) {
280:             bail!(EzError::BranchNotInStack(branch_name.clone()));
281:         }
282: 
283:         set_branch_value(&mut state, &branch_name, &effective_key, value)?;
284:         state.save()?;
285: 
286:         ui::success(&format!(
287:             "{branch_name}.{effective_key} = {value}"
288:         ));
289: 
290:         ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
291:             "cmd": "config set",
292:             "key": effective_key,
293:             "value": value,
294:             "branch": branch_name,
295:         }));
296:     } else {
297:         if !is_known_global_key(&effective_key) {
298:             bail!(EzError::UserMessage(format!(
299:                 "unknown config key `{effective_key}`\n  → Known keys: {}\n  → Branch attrs: {}",
300:                 KNOWN_KEYS
301:                     .iter()
302:                     .map(|(k, _)| *k)
303:                     .collect::<Vec<_>>()
304:                     .join(", "),
305:                 BRANCH_KEYS
306:                     .iter()
307:                     .map(|(k, _)| *k)
308:                     .collect::<Vec<_>>()
309:                     .join(", "),
310:             )));
311:         }
312: 
313:         let old_value = get_global_value(&state, &effective_key);
314:         set_global_value(&mut state, &effective_key, value)?;
315:         state.save()?;
316: 
317:         match old_value {
318:             Some(old) if old != value => {
319:                 ui::success(&format!("{effective_key}: {old} → {value}"));
320:             }
321:             Some(_) => {
322:                 ui::info(&format!("{effective_key} is already set to `{value}`"));
323:             }
324:             None => {
325:                 ui::success(&format!("{effective_key} = {value}"));
326:             }
327:         }
328: 
329:         ui::receipt(&serde_json::json!({
330:             "cmd": "config set",
331:             "key": effective_key,
332:             "value": value,
333:         }));
334:     }
335: 
336:     Ok(())
337: }
338: 
339: // ── Global value helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
340: 
341: fn get_global_value(state: &StackState, key: &str) -> Option<String> {
342:     match key {
343:         "trunk" => Some(state.trunk.clone()),
344:         "remote" => Some(state.remote.clone()),
345:         "default_from" => state.default_from.clone(),
346:         "repo" => state.repo.clone(),
347:         "draft" => state.draft.map(|v| v.to_string()),
348:         "no_pr" => state.no_pr.map(|v| v.to_string()),
349:         "rerere" => state.rerere.map(|v| v.to_string()),
350:         _ => None,
351:     }
352: }
353: 
354: fn set_global_value(state: &mut StackState, key: &str, value: &str) -> Result<()> {
355:     if is_bool_key(key) {
356:         let _ = parse_bool(value)?;
357:     }
358: 
359:     match key {
360:         "trunk" => {
361:             state.trunk = value.to_string();
362:         }
363:         "remote" => {
364:             state.remote = value.to_string();
365:         }
366:         "default_from" => {
367:             state.default_from = Some(value.to_string());
368:         }
369:         "repo" => {
370:             state.repo = Some(value.to_string());
371:         }
372:         "draft" => {
373:             state.draft = Some(parse_bool(value)?);
374:         }
375:         "no_pr" => {
376:             state.no_pr = Some(parse_bool(value)?);
377:         }
378:         "rerere" => {
379:             let enabled = parse_bool(value)?;
380:             state.rerere = Some(enabled);
381:             if enabled {
382:                 enable_rerere();
383:             }
384:         }
385:         _ => {
386:             bail!(EzError::UserMessage(format!("unknown config key `{key}`")));
387:         }
388:     }
389:     Ok(())
390: }
391: 
392: // ── Branch value helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
393: 
394: fn get_branch_value(meta: &crate::stack::BranchMeta, key: &str) -> Option<String> {
395:     match key {
396:         "pr" => {
397:             // Return a composite: repo#number or just number
398:             match (&meta.pr_repo, meta.pr_number) {
399:                 (Some(repo), Some(num)) => Some(format!("{repo}#{num}")),
400:                 (Some(repo), None) => Some(repo.clone()),
401:                 (None, Some(num)) => Some(num.to_string()),
402:                 (None, None) => None,
403:             }
404:         }
405:         "pr_repo" => meta.pr_repo.clone(),
406:         "pr_number" => meta.pr_number.map(|n| n.to_string()),
407:         "push_remote" => meta.push_remote.clone(),
408:         "parent" => Some(meta.parent.clone()),
409:         "scope" => meta.scope.as_ref().map(|s| s.join(",")),
410:         "scope_mode" => meta.scope_mode.map(|m| match m {
411:             crate::stack::ScopeMode::Warn => "warn".to_string(),
412:             crate::stack::ScopeMode::Strict => "strict".to_string(),
413:         }),
414:         // Also allow reading per-branch `remote` as alias for push_remote
415:         "remote" => meta.push_remote.clone(),
416:         _ => None,
417:     }
418: }
419: 
420: fn set_branch_value(
421:     state: &mut StackState,
422:     branch: &str,
423:     key: &str,
424:     value: &str,
425: ) -> Result<()> {
426:     match key {
427:         "pr" => {
428:             // Smart parser
429:             let parsed = parse_pr_value(value);
430:             if parsed.repo.is_none() && parsed.number.is_none() {
431:                 bail!(EzError::UserMessage(format!(
432:                     "could not parse PR value `{value}`\n  → Accepted formats: 123, #123, owner/repo#123, owner/repo, URL"
433:                 )));
434:             }
435:             let meta = state.get_branch_mut(branch)?;
436:             if let Some(ref repo) = parsed.repo {
437:                 meta.pr_repo = Some(repo.clone());
438:             }
439:             if let Some(num) = parsed.number {
440:                 meta.pr_number = Some(num);
441:             }
442:         }
443:         "pr_repo" => {
444:             let repo = parse_pr_repo_value(value);
445:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_repo = Some(repo);
446:         }
447:         "pr_number" => {
448:             let num: u64 = value.parse().map_err(|_| {
449:                 EzError::UserMessage(format!(
450:                     "invalid PR number `{value}` — must be a positive integer"
451:                 ))
452:             })?;
453:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.pr_number = Some(num);
454:         }
455:         "push_remote" | "remote" => {
456:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.push_remote = Some(value.to_string());
457:         }
458:         "parent" => {
459:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.parent = value.to_string();
460:         }
461:         "scope" => {
462:             let patterns: Vec<String> = value
463:                 .split(',')
464:                 .map(|s| s.trim().to_string())
465:                 .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
466:                 .collect();
467:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.scope = if patterns.is_empty() {
468:                 None
469:             } else {
470:                 Some(patterns)
471:             };
472:         }
473:         "scope_mode" => {
474:             let mode = match value.to_lowercase().as_str() {
475:                 "warn" => crate::stack::ScopeMode::Warn,
476:                 "strict" => crate::stack::ScopeMode::Strict,
477:                 _ => bail!(EzError::UserMessage(format!(
478:                     "invalid scope_mode `{value}` — use warn or strict"
479:                 ))),
480:             };
481:             state.get_branch_mut(branch)?.scope_mode = Some(mode);
482:         }
483:         _ => {
484:             bail!(EzError::UserMessage(format!(
485:                 "unknown branch attribute `{key}`\n  → Known attrs: {}",
486:                 BRANCH_KEYS
487:                     .iter()
488:                     .map(|(k, _)| *k)
489:                     .collect::<Vec<_>>()
490:                     .join(", ")
491:             )));
492:         }
493:     }
494:     Ok(())
495: }
496: 
497: /// Enable git rerere. Falls back to creating .git/rr-cache if git config fails.
498: fn enable_rerere() {
499:     let config_ok = std::process::Command::new("git")
500:         .args(["config", "rerere.enabled", "true"])
501:         .status()
502:         .map(|s| s.success())
503:         .unwrap_or(false);
504: 
505:     let autoupdate_ok = std::process::Command::new("git")
506:         .args(["config", "rerere.autoupdate", "true"])
507:         .status()
508:         .map(|s| s.success())
509:         .unwrap_or(false);
510: 
511:     if !config_ok || !autoupdate_ok {
512:         if let Ok(git_dir) = crate::git::git_common_dir() {
513:             let rr_cache = git_dir.join("rr-cache");
514:             if let Err(e) = std::fs::create_dir_all(&rr_cache) {
515:                 crate::ui::warn(&format!("Could not create rr-cache directory: {e}"));
516:             } else {
517:                 crate::ui::warn(
518:                     "Could not set git config — created rr-cache directory directly",
519:                 );
520:             }
521:         }
522:     }
523: }

532:         let repo = init_git_repo("config-test");

535:             .save()

541:         let repo = init_git_repo("config-branch-test");
542:         let cwd = CwdGuard::enter(&repo);
543:         let mut state = StackState::new("main".to_string());

756:     #[test]
757:     fn backward_compat_loads_old_state_without_new_fields() {
758:         let _guard = take_env_lock();
759:         let repo = init_git_repo("config-compat");
760:         let _cwd = CwdGuard::enter(&repo);
761: 
762:         let dir = StackState::meta_dir().unwrap();
763:         std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).unwrap();
764:         let old_json = r#"{
765:             "trunk": "main",
766:             "remote": "origin",
767:             "branches": {}
768:         }"#;
769:         std::fs::write(StackState::state_path().unwrap(), old_json).unwrap();
770: 
771:         let state = StackState::load().expect("should load old format");
772:         assert_eq!(state.trunk, "main");
773:         assert_eq!(state.remote, "origin");
774:         assert_eq!(state.default_from, None);
775:         assert_eq!(state.repo, None);
776:         assert_eq!(state.draft, None);
777:         assert_eq!(state.no_pr, None);
778:         assert_eq!(state.rerere, None);
779:     }

786: 
787:         let dir = StackState::meta_dir().unwrap();
788:         std::fs::create_dir_all(&dir).unwrap();
789:         let old_json = r#"{
790:             "trunk": "main",
791:             "remote": "origin",
792:             "branches": {
793:                 "feat/old": {
794:                     "name": "feat/old",
795:                     "parent": "main",
796:                     "parent_head": "abc123",
797:                     "pr_number": 42
798:                 }
799:             }
800:         }"#;
801:         std::fs::write(StackState::state_path().unwrap(), old_json).unwrap();
802: 
803:         let state = StackState::load().expect("should load old branch format");
804:         let branch = state.get_branch("feat/old").expect("branch should exist");
805:         assert_eq!(branch.pr_number, Some(42));
806:         assert_eq!(branch.pr_repo, None, "pr_repo should default to None for old branches");
807:     }
